title: Rich Sour Ethiopian Lard Shiro Wot
description: Shiro Wot, a classic Ethiopian one-pot dish, is made richer with the addition of lard and a tangy lacquer glaze. This Mediterranean-friendly recipe serves 4-6 people as a main course.

ingredients.name: Lard
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality, rendered lard for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Finely chop the onion to prevent it from overpowering the dish.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced garlic is essential for the flavor of Shiro Wot.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Fresh ginger adds a nice warmth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Berbere spice
ingredients.notes: Berbere spice is a staple in Ethiopian cuisine, giving Shiro Wot its signature flavor.

ingredients.name: Teff flour
ingredients.notes: Teff flour helps to thicken the sauce and add texture.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Use filtered water for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Lacquer glaze
ingredients.notes: A tangy and sweet lacquer glaze is the perfect finishing touch.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Add salt to taste, as the flavor of Shiro Wot can be quite strong.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Black pepper adds a nice depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Clarified butter
ingredients.notes: Clarified butter is essential for the rich flavor of Shiro Wot.

ingredients.name: Fresh cilantro
ingredients.notes: Fresh cilantro adds a bright and refreshing touch to the dish.

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Fresh parsley pairs well with the flavors of Shiro Wot.

instructions: Heat the lard in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the finely chopped onion and cook until it's translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the Berbere spice and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the spice is fragrant.
Add the teff flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until the flour is lightly toasted.
Gradually add the water, whisking constantly to prevent lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the clarified butter and salt.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Transfer the Shiro Wot to a serving platter and drizzle with the lacquer glaze.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and parsley.
